title: Natick police officer on leave following domestic violence charges
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime “Things in this [case] are not what they seem to be,” an attorney for Jackson Dwyer said.

By Abby Patkin April 29, 2026 | 1:05 PM 2 minutes to read A Natick police officer is on paid leave after he allegedly suffocated his fiancée with a pillow during an argument at their home in Holliston Saturday, according to court records.
Jackson Dwyer, 28, pleaded not guilty Monday to charges of assault and battery on a family or household member, strangulation or suffocation, and witness intimidation.

title: 11 arrested outside Burlington ICE facility in act of ‘civil disobedience,’ police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News The anti-ICE protesters refused to relocate to a "free speech area" that was identified by Burlington police.
An ICE car parked outside the agency's field office in Burlington in September 2025.

title: What are you reading at the beach for summer 2026?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Books Beach bags are not complete without a good book — or several.
What are you reading this summer?
Craig St Andre enjoys reading a Stephen King book seated on his chair with plenty of room at Wollaston Beach in Quincy.

title: Joel Embiid’s return is giving the 76ers a level of confidence that could make them dangerous
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics "When he wants to be dominant, he will be," Tyrese Maxey said.
Joel Embiid dunks against the Celtics in the 76ers' Game 5 win.

title: Pressley joins Epstein survivors demanding meeting with King Charles
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News The king declined to meet with survivors as he visited Washington to mark the 250th anniversary of America's independence from Britain.

title: Mega Millions ticket sales take a hit after price jumps to $5
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News Pricier tickets and lower jackpots are dragging sales down 25% from last year.
A Mega Millions lottery ticket is printed out of a lottery machine at Mares Mart in Chicago, Sunday, April 6, 2025.

title: Before Bruins-Sabres Game 5, Buffalo fans rescued the Canadian anthem after singer’s mic cut out
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Bruins It's the latest in a proud tradition of hockey fans occasionally singing the anthem on their own.
Fans cheer Bowen Byram of the Sabres as he warms up prior to Game 5 in Buffalo.

Joe Hrycych/Getty Images By Hayden Bird April 29, 2026 | 10:46 AM 3 minutes to read Buffalo fans made the most out of a tech glitch: Before the puck dropped in Game 5 of the Bruins-Sabres first-round playoff series on Tuesday, an unprompted display of international unity briefly seemed to transcend hockey.

title: Fundraiser started for daughter of woman killed, allegedly by husband, at UMass Amherst
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The funds will be directed to the daughter's grandparents to ensure she receives necessary counseling and to help secure her future.
Emma MacDonald, 31, was found dead inside a room at Hotel UMass last week.
Courtesy of GoFundMe Previously: Many are stepping up to support the young daughter of a woman who was killed last week at UMass Amherst, allegedly at the hands of her own husband.

title: Mike Minogue isn’t the right choice for Massachusetts, readers say. Here’s why.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Readers Say “What happened to centrist Republicans that understand the people and government?
Businessmen just keep putting profit over people.
” Mike Minogue candidate for Governor spoke to the media briefly at the Massachusetts GOP Convention in Worcester, MA on Saturday April, 25 2026.
